# Autonomous Codex Execution Pack

ACEP is the final package generated by Project Blueprint Engine.

It is designed for Codex to read and execute without further user questions until a stop condition is reached.

## Autonomy Level

The default MVP autonomy level is `autonomous_until_stop`.

This means Codex continues through task cards without asking the user unless a stop condition occurs.

## Generated Files

ACEP creates this virtual folder:

```text
.pbe/
  codex-execution-pack/
    00-readme.md
    01-autonomous-execution-policy.md
    02-project-blueprint.md
    03-requirement-tree.md
    04-work-roadmap.md
    05-verification-plan.md
    06-codex-operating-loop.md
    07-task-cards/
      task-001.md
      task-002.md
    08-validation-commands.md
    09-completion-criteria.md
    10-failure-recovery.md
    11-final-report-template.md
    execution-manifest.json
```

## Handoff Prompt

The app generates a top-level prompt instructing Codex to read `.pbe/codex-execution-pack/`, follow `execution-manifest.json`, use the operating loop, obey each task card's scope and non-scope, record evidence, and write the final report.

## Preview And Export

The MVP supports:

- virtual file list preview
- individual ACEP file content preview
- full bundle preview with file paths and contents
- Markdown bundle export
- `execution-manifest.json` export
- virtual file list export
- bundle preview export

## Stop Conditions

Codex must stop when a task requires credentials, deployment, destructive migration, out-of-scope work, or when the same validation failure repeats three times.
